productTitle: EarFun Free 1S Wireless Earbuds, 4 Mics ENC Bluetooth Earbuds for Clear Call, 7mm Composite Drivers, Powerful Sound, App for Custom EQ, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Charging, Game Mode, 30H
reviewTitle: Works well but hope you don't have an issue with it
reviewText: Nice headphones with good sound. Unfortunately after 6 month's use, the case would not charge and it would just flash the white LEDs erratically and quickly.  If you look this up, this does seem to be an issue for some people.  I tried everything, obvious things such as swapping the cable - in case my cable was damaged.  Of course, the problem was with the case and not the cable!  This is where I bring in the negative part of the review.  You do not want to engage with Earfun customer services.  The help I got was initially a video showing me how to plug the cable into the back of the case! Fair enough that there may be a small minority of the population who may not know this but I think most people know how to put the cable in so that it charges.  Especially someone who is going to use wireless earphones! I, again, reiterated that the case was not charging and received a response that I would have to take a video of the cable and charger showing that it was not charging and then attach the same cable and charger to another device to prove that the charger and cable were working and film another video.  Then I would have to repeat the process twice with a different cable and video - so a total of 4 videos to send! It was at this point that I complained to Amazon about Earfun's customer service.  Thankfully, Amazon have top notch customer service and were able to resolve the issue for me.  In conclusion, yes, they are nice earphones but if you have an issue with them, prepare to send emails and numerous videos to their head office in China and hope that they will resolve it for you! I will not buy Earfun products again - not due to their sound quality but customer service.  It is a well known fault and they essentially put you off making a warranty claim.

productTitle: EarFun Free 1S Wireless Earbuds, 4 Mics ENC Bluetooth Earbuds for Clear Call, 7mm Composite Drivers, Powerful Sound, App for Custom EQ, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Charging, Game Mode, 30H

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: (I've updated this review twice, eventually arriving at 5 stars - see the updates at the end)Firstly, these buds have excellent sound for the price. They're not quite as well rounded as the audiophile type headphones, but they are really very, very good. They're certainly plenty good enough for a run around the block, or the commute to work. The sound isolation by virtue of being an earplug design is good, but I can still hear enough around me to be situationally aware.The charge case is nice too - and seems to provide plenty of power for a good long time. The buds are held in by a little magnet so there's a clear "in" and "out". The stalks go into two deep holes which have springy contacts in them for charging. Those holes are possibly a problem if some fluff gets in there, or if you need to clean the contacts.There are some wrinkles though - hardly surprising given the price. Firstly, skipping tacks, pause/resume, adjusting the volume etc all require a number of "taps" with a finger in a particular ear. This is okay, but it's prone to problems mis-counting the number of taps you intend. If your finger doesn't go right onto the bud, it may not be counted, if you go too quick, it may not be counted, if it's a Tuesday, it may not be counted... you get the idea. This has lead to more than a few pauses when I wanted to skip, volume when I wanted to pause and I've lost count of how many times it's told me it's switching to "game mode" (which is missing a consonant, so sounds more like "gay mode").Also, when putting the buds back into the charger, sometimes things don't work right. First it was the left one, now the right - when I put them in, sometimes they charge (and disconnect bluetooth), sometimes they just stay there. If the latter, then they'll eventually run out of battery, and re-connecting them to bluetooth seems overly hit-and-miss. Some days it takes several attempts to get both ears to work at the same time.When this first happened, I contacted Earfun, who it's fair to say were great. They told me to clean the contacts (not so easy down the bottom of the tube in the charger), and push hard and whatnot. On that occasion it worked, but subsequent times it's been a lot less successful. My guess is the actual buds get themselves in a mess and don't start to charge/disconnect when they should, and so need some "resetting" to make them behave. The little LED on the buds sometimes does some very weird things, and sometimes the buds seem to try to talk but garble instead. There's no reset button (or apparently a way to switch them off/on or whatever), so getting them back to themselves isn't always easy. Sometimes you just have to let them run out of battery and then recharge them to get them to work. This all smells of a software problem on the buds (not upgradeable), so I hope Earfun can sort it for a subsequent model.All in all though, these are great, and I would recommend them to anyone looking for a decent set of buds (and Earfun themselves look to be good). The problems with them are very annoying but not a show-stopper (for me at least). Not quite "apple" yet, but then they're like a 10th of the price.UPDATE: Earfun have got in contact to smooth over the above (was a 3 star review). Adding a star because that's unexpected from them, is above and beyond what I'd ever reasonably expect and another clear plus point for these buds.Update 2: (Moved up to 5 stars) Earfun replaced the old with the newer model (the one with app connectivity, and it comes with a little cleaning stick). The app is plenty good enough (probably won't need it more than once or twice), and thankfully doesn't need you to register to use it. I've more or less left my buds as 'default', except to turn off Game Mode. The app provides a way to update the earbuds firmware, so I guess this is a way for Earfun to improve what they can without needing to replace the hardware.Further, the actual buds work better than before. The finger touch thing seems more 'definite' than before, so a lot less pauses when I meant skip, or whatever. They seem to pair with my watch more reliably than before too, and so far have charged flawlessly. Earfun seemed to think previous problems were with dirty contacts, and have provided a little cleaning stick this time, so time will tell if that's still a problem here. So far, the buds seem to disconnect and charge as they ought to, so I don't expect random 'battery low' issues (the app can tell you the charge of each bud too - if you need such a thing).All in all then, 5 stars as they sound great, work well and are at a price you can't beat. Earfun service has been excellent too.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: These are great value. I bought a couple of pairs of these a while ago for my kids as a budget alternative to Apple AirPods. I borrowed my son’s and I was so impressed by the sound quality I bought myself a pair too.They fit my ears far better than AirPods and don’t fall out at all - which AirPods seem to do all the time (especially when I’m running) and because of how well they fit, these block out external sound far better (they’re not noise cancelling, but the rubber ear tips do a great job of creating a seal in your ear to block out external sound).Unlike AirPods they can only connect with and remember one device at time. But reconnecting is very easy with the button on the charge case.They hold their charge really well and last a long time. I have no reason at the moment to doubt the 35+ hour claim on the box, but I’ve come no where near testing them for this long.Sound is good, although I actually find them a bit too bassy with some tracks - but this only makes me lower the volume, which can’t be a bad thing.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Great battery life, a bit uncomfortable.
reviewText: Amazing value, really solid pair of earbuds. In particular, the battery life is great and they connect and disconnect easily. Mic seems good and nice sound.I have found them a little uncomfortable to wear though. Sometimes, after an hour or so, I have to take them out. I have tried a few different sizes to the squidgy bits that probably have a name, but no good fit for long term use so far.I saw some reviews complaining the touch controls were too sensitive. I have the opposite problem. Pausing and playing a podcast with a double tap sometimes works and sometimes doesn't. I've found two very firm taps works best. But it is annoying as it takes me 5-6 seconds to get it to pause sometimes and with each failed attempt I'm often changing the volume (single tap).Also, for those other single bud-at-a-timers, volume up is on 1 bud, and volume down is on the other, so you won't be able to effectively use the volume controls when using only 1 bud.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: I did some research before purchasing these earbuds and have to say that so far with a week or so of use have been very happy with the performance.  As noted elsewhere,  the bass is a tad forward but not unpleasantly so. With most audiophile equipment, a burn in period is to be expected.  These, right off the bat performed better than I had expected.  To my ears the treble is fine - clear and crisp, providing a fairly well defined sound stage that belies their modest price. Battery life compares well with more premium buds and range is equally good. I have no need for noise cancelling so do not miss this feature and currently rate these for audio, fitment and battery life to be at least equal to my premium earbuds. Absolutely excellent value to performance ratio!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Until I read the What HiFi 5 Star review, I didn’t know anything about Earfun products. My £180 Apple AirPods are giving up; 30 mins and the right hand, errr, “pod” gives up… and the left one follows 10 mins later. Do I want to spend another £200 on a pair of EarPods when these are somewhat more economical? No. So I bought these and theybare…. they are FANTASTIC! They’re as good as the Californian (Chinese) products; just as well made, are brilliantly designed, have a longer life, stay in your ear comfortably, charge wirelessly, are perfect to use in the gym as they’re IPX7 rated… and that means ensures that the device can be submerged in water up to 1 meter for 30 minutes without any damage - and finally and most importantly, they sound absolutely superb. Just buy them before everyone finds out!

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Ok sound, reliable connection and great battery life
reviewText: The case is much bulkier than expected, easily twice or three times the size of an AirPods case, not that easily pocketable.The sound is ok, I expected better. It's quite middle heavy, bass is not too bad but not much treble. It's excellent for calls and microphones seem to work well, not that great for music.It still deserves 5 stars from me if I factor in the price as setup was dead easy, it connects reliably, presented well in the box and the build quality seems good.Battery life is excellent, it is replacement for a dying pair of AirPods that only lasts 15 minutes after as little as 2 years of very light use.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Great price, especially when on sale. Good sound though the fit can be a bit tricky. Worth trying the different tips.  Once you get them snug then bass and lower registers sounds much much better. Have bought two pairs already for kids and they are big fans. Another pair, maybe two will be purchased soon enough I’d say for others in the house.All in all a solid buy that are much better quality than the price suggests

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: **Updated... my earbuds were faulty and inrecieved a full refund (an offer of exchange was also made). Customer service for this company is OUTSTANDING. They reached out to me. I didn't have to do anything. I'd buy from them again and just hope I was unlucky with my previous product.**Ok. So these are a great price. They sound amazing! Way better than any other ear buds I've owned (maybe even better than my galaxy buds FE) and for me what's great is that they don't FALL OUT when I'm working out. They just stay in with the biggest ear buds attachment (other sizes included). They last last for ages on one charge (don't think I've I've even recharged the case yet!). HOWEVER, it's really p***ING me off that every time I use them I have to go to go through the whole pairing process every time! And sometimes they doesn't even work and they just don't connect. This is with a Samsung tab, s23 phone, and steam deck oled. It's really annoying to the point I'm using my old headphones, so what was the point in buying need ones? Waste of money I'm sad to say.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: I’d live to have the money for some high end headphones like Bose etc, but wanted something I could use as hands free in the car. These came out well in reviews so decided to give them a go. They’re perfectly fine for the purpose, although the mic picks up a lot of background noise (I’m told) but I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of sound whilst playing music. They won’t compete with the high end products, but then they’re not £200. I now use them more for working out or when travelling and I’m very happy with them. They certainly feel like a more expensive set with good quality plastic and a decent feel, way better than my last unbranded set which were half the price but a 10th of the quality. If you’re on a budget and don’t expect studio quality sound, these are worth a fo

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Well if youre reading reviews and thinking £26 must be no good well these buds punch way above their price and at rrp£50 would still be a steal.I read loads of reviews before taking the plunge and so glad i chose these set up in seconds and auto blutooth to phone everytime  and they sound amazing from spotify not using for phone calls only music but you will not be dosapointed and skipping tracks or adjusting volume via tap on left or right bud easy. Charging and battery life at 40hrs WOW. Very comfortable  and supplied with 5 different sets of ear pads. Why pay £££s when these 5star buds are just perfect.Highly recomend.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Constant problems but fast response from manufacturer
reviewText: I have had some kind of issue with these since day 1. The left earbud doesn't work very well. Most of the time when I try to use these in the morning I find that only the right headphone connects and though it is possible to solve this issue it is a frustrating 5 minutes of constantly attempting to pair them until it works.It also seems that once I've managed to connect to it, the left earbud wants to keep connecting even when it is in the charging case. Frustrating again when someone calls me and I miss it because the audio is only coming out of one earbud safely tucked away in its case where it is meant to be charging and not connected to my phone.Finally, they have recently become fully unusable thanks to the left earbud refusing to charge past about 10%. They last about 10 mins of my walk now before they start disrupting my music with "battery low... disconnected.... pairing... disconnected... pairing... battery low... disconnected... pairing... power off"UPDATE: The manufacturer was very responsive when I emailed and are sending a new pair. For now I am updating the review to 4 stars, which may further change depending on how the new pair perform.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Love these buds, very comfy fit great quality sound. They appear and look great. Agree with What Hi Fi said about them. Easy to operate excellent quality product.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Can’t believe how good these are for the money
reviewText: After misplacing my AirPods yet again, I decided to opt for a much cheaper alternative so I went with the whatHiFi recommendation and I genuinely can’t believe how good these sound. There easy to operate and the sound is superb for cheap earbuds. They also don’t fall out my ears the way the AirPods often did. Brilliant find for the money. Great sound and good bass.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: These were recommended to me and as they are relatively cheap I decided to buy them. Easy to link to phone, comfortable, absolute bargain for the price.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: Bought these after seeing their review in What HiFi. I needed to replace my running headphones that had given up after a couple of years of service and these sounded OK. Got a pair and I quite like them. But....I have found that they will not stay in my ears whilst running. Have been out a few times since getting them and they just won't stay in. The left one will pop out every hundred metres and the right every five hundred. It's annoying and frustrating. I've tried all of the ear tips but none of them help. I've even tried some from other headphones but I just cannot get them to fit well enough to stay in my ear. I am a bit of a sweaty runner so perhaps the added lubrication there is the cause. Maybe I have unusually large earholes. Who knows. I'd just say they are not really for running. For walking and less strenuous stuff they're perfectly fine. The sound is not the best, but I've paid much more for headphones that sound a lot worse. They're a bit heavy on the base but again, it's not like they're couple of hundred pounds and designed for audiophiles. I'd say they were great value for money and good enough for day-to-day use, just not for running.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Overall pretty decent for the money.
reviewText: You can certainly get cheaper and of course more expensive but these are good for the money, the touch controls work pretty well, something I had trouble with on a cheaper set and the sound quality is excellent, you may not think so if you're a bit of an audiophile.I've used them for an hour at the gym with no issues but early today, I was pottering around the house and they started to drop connection, I've got them paired to my Apple Watch so it wasn't a distance issue, and it hadn't been much more than and hour. I've popped them back on charge and I'll try them again, hopefully this isn't a sign of them starting to let me down as I'm otherwise very happy with them.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewTitle: Awkward fitting and sensitivity and a terrible app
reviewText: The sound quality is pretty good (not 5* What Hifi) good strong bass. Ive tried all sizes of buds. None fit that well. Once fitted the odd shape means that do not hang vertically but the stems point directly forwards, and quite frankly they look ugly. Others have mentioned the annoying over sensitive touch pads. It is impossible to insert them without turning on an unwanted feature (game mode on, game mode off - in an annoying Chinese voice). Also they pair too eagerly and turn back on your music track that you have just closed. Once you get used to them they perform reasonably well and the volume control works. Pause and skip are a little more tricky. Unless you have an equaliser on your phone you will be disappointed with the Earfun app - it is absolutely abysmal with 4 presets that make almost no difference. I will keep them however as the sound is good. But I will only use them indoors as they look awful and fall out easily. A wired pair work best outside. A good effort Earfun, but these are a work in progress.

productTitle: EarFun Wireless Earbuds, [What Hi-Fi Awards] Air Bluetooth In-Ear Headphones with 4 Mics ENC, Sweatshield™ IPX7 Waterproof, Clear Sound, Deep Bass, Wireless Charge, App Customize EQ, 35H, White
reviewText: These in ear buds are remarkably good and excellent value for money. Pairing is simple and battery life is fine. Recommended at this price.

productTitle: Apple AirPods with wired Charging Case (2nd generation)
reviewText: Provide quite solid audio quality. Battery life isn’t bad either🔋 and connectivity with other Apple devices is absolutely top-notch.On the downside, they’re expensive, and possess mediocre noise-cancelling. They certainly don’t fit everyone!

productTitle: Apple AirPods with wired Charging Case (2nd generation)
reviewText: OMG These are a definite game changer for me.  I’ve tried so many different ones and couldn’t find any to fit me not without falling out.  But a lady at work suggested these ones and I tried them on and WOW THEY DID NOT FALL OUT.  And at the gym today they didn’t fall out.  Had a jog on the treadmill and boy oh boy so comfortable.  I forgot they were in.  Definitely worth the money.  Thanks apple .🍏
